title: Hachnasat Orchim: The Art of Jewish Hospitality
title_zh: 待客之道：犹太人的款待艺术
author: Torah Light Writer
key_concepts: Hospitality as greater than receiving Divine Presence
chinese_cultural_parallels: Analects quote: 'Is it not delightful to have friends coming from distant quarters?'
Shared emphasis on respect for guests and sharing resources
historical_period: Patriarchal Era (~1800 BCE) / Xia Dynasty (夏朝)
image_description: Chinese brushstroke (shan shui) style landscape showing a warm, welcoming home with an open door and light streaming out into the evening, with travelers approaching on a winding path through gentle mountains
